questions.text: Which of the following best explains why copper is an electrical conductor but rubber is not?

answerChoices.text: In copper, valence electrons are shared amongst all nuclei, but in rubber valence electrons are held by specific nuclei.
In rubber, valence electrons are shared amongst all nuclei, but in copper valence electrons are held by specific nuclei.
In both copper and rubber, valence electrons are shared amongst all nuclei, but they have more freedom to move in copper.
In both copper and rubber, valence electrons are held by specific nuclei, but they are held more strongly in rubber.

questions.text: For the circuit pictured below, which is the best estimate of the time it will take a single electron to move around the entire circuit from the positive end of the battery to the negative end?

answerChoices.text: Several hours
A nanosecond
A few minutes
A few seconds

questions.text: When a circuit is connected, the light bulb turns on immediately. Which statement below best explains why?

answerChoices.text: The battery pushes electrons already present in the light bulb's filament.
The electrons move at the speed of light, so they reach the light bulb immediately.
It actually takes about two seconds for the bulb to turn on, but your brain tricks you because it has seen so many light bulbs turn on.
It takes a couple of minutes for the electrons to reach the light bulb, but the light bulb has some energy stored from the last time it was one.

questions.text: In the circuit pictured below, which direction are the electrons moving, and which direction does the current (as it is mathematically defined) flow?

answerChoices.text: The electrons move counterclockwise and the current moves clockwise.
The current moves counterclockwise and the electrons move clockwise.
Both the electrons and the current move counterclockwise.
Both the current and the electrons move clockwise.

questions.text: Consider an electrical wire connected to a battery in which a current is flowing. Is it positively charged, negatively charged, or neutral and why?

answerChoices.text: It is neutral because the battery supplies voltage, not charge.
It must be negative because it is full of electrons supplied by the battery.
It begins negative, because one side of the battery supplies electrons, but it becomes positive as the other side of the battery consumes them.
The charge of the electrical wire is unpredictable because electrons are in random motion.

questions.text: Electrons in a circuit are often depicted moving in a straight line. This is a good, but not a perfect illustration of how electrons move.

answerChoices.text: They are moving randomly while slowly drifting in a single direction.
The are jumping from one atom to another in quick bursts of energy.
They are bouncing off the walls of the circuit as they fly around it very fast.
Only electrons supplied by the battery move. Most of the electrons in the wire are not moving.
